metadata.title: Hamilton Microlab STAR Digital Twin Specification
metadata.description: Machine-readable specification of the Hamilton Microlab STAR liquid handling system at the firmware command level. Maps physical hardware modules to firmware commands to VENUS software steps. Designed for digital twin simulation and accessible to both humans and coding agents.

command_format.description: All commands sent as ASCII strings to Master Module (C0)
command_format.module_prefix: 2 uppercase characters identifying the target module
command_format.parameters: 2 lowercase chars + value, order flexible after id

deck.description: Flat work surface with track-based positioning

coordinate_system.x_axis: Left-right along the arm rail (0.1mm units in FW)
coordinate_system.y_axis: Front-back perpendicular to arm (0.1mm units in FW)

components.tracks: Parallel slots at 22.5mm pitch for carrier placement
components.carriers: Removable trays holding labware, identified by barcode
components.labware: Plates, tubes, tip racks placed on carriers
components.tip_waste: Collection station for used disposable tips

error_codes.03: Command not completed (error in previous sequence)
error_codes.09: No carrier (load command without carrier present)
error_codes.10: Not completed (aborted due to prior error)
error_codes.11: Dispense with pressure LLD not permitted
error_codes.14: Sequenced aspiration with pressure LLD not permitted
error_codes.25: Illegal user access (carrier removed or cover open)
error_codes.27: Position not reachable (out of mechanical limits)
error_codes.28: Unexpected LLD (liquid level before scan started)
error_codes.99: Slave error (error in one of the slaves)

master.name: Master Module
master.description: Central controller on the LAN Dual Processor Board. Receives all commands from the host computer via USB/Ethernet and orchestrates slave modules over CAN bus. All operational commands (pipetting, transport, loading) are addressed to C0.

pipetting_channels.name: Pipetting Channels (1000 uL / PIP)
pipetting_channels.description: Up to 16 independent 1000uL air-displacement pipetting channels. Each channel has independent Y and Z drives. Tips attached via CO-RE compression O-ring technology. Supports cLLD, pLLD, MAD, ADC, TADM monitoring.

DI.name: Initialize Pipetting Channels
DI.description: Initialize PIP channels, discard any fitted tips. Must be called before any pipetting.

AS.description: Aspiration of liquid using PIP channels. Supports multiple aspiration types, LLD modes, TADM monitoring, and mixing.

DS.name: Dispense

venus_steps.description: VENUS software steps organized in three tiers: Power Steps (highest-level wizards), Easy Steps (composite operations), and Single Steps (direct FW command wrappers). Each tier decomposes into the one below.

architecture.flow: VENUS Method (HSL) -> Step -> Run_* class -> McX* method -> AtsMcX* class -> FW command (C0XX)

power_steps.description: Highest-level wizard-based steps with full pipetting workflow configuration

TransferSamples.description: Transfer samples between source and destination with full tip/asp/disp/eject cycle

AddReagent.description: Add reagent from trough to multiple destinations

SerialDilution.description: Perform serial dilution across a plate

LoadAndMatch.description: Load carriers and match with worklist

easy_steps.description: Composite steps that combine tip handling + liquid handling into one operation

single_steps.description: Direct FW command wrappers — each maps to exactly one firmware command

can_nodes.description: CAN bus node name assignments for dynamic slave modules

roadmap.description: Planned extensions for the digital twin specification

phases.name: Core Workflow Complete
phases.scope: Master, PIP channels, 96 Head, AutoLoad, iSWAP, CO-RE Gripper, Wash Station, TCC

phases.name: Full Command Detail for All Modules
phases.scope: Expand all stubbed modules with full parameter detail from FW command docs

phases.name: Deck Layout and Labware Model
phases.scope: Track positions, carrier types, labware definitions, sequence handling

phases.name: Liquid Class Model

phases.name: CAD Integration

phases.name: Simulation Engine

deck_layout.description: The deck is the flat work surface where carriers holding labware are placed. It uses a track-based positioning system with 22.5mm pitch. Carriers slide into tracks either manually or via AutoLoad.

track_system.description: Parallel slots at 22.5mm spacing. Carriers occupy 1 to 7 tracks.

coordinate_mapping.note: Exact offset depends on instrument calibration. FW uses 0.1mm units.

identification.method: Barcode label on back of carrier

TIP_CAR_480_NTR.description: Tip carrier for Nested Tip Racks (4-layer stacking)

PLT_CAR_L5MD.description: Plate carrier, 5 positions, landscape, medium density

PLT_CAR_L5HD.description: Plate carrier, 5 positions, landscape, high density

MultiFlex.description: Modular carrier base for up to 5 ANSI/SLAS modules

Shaker_Carrier_Base.description: Base for Hamilton Heater Shaker + modules

Active_Carrier_Base.description: Plug-in base for Active Plate Nest and ApH Module

CVS_Carrier.description: Crystal Vacuum System carrier with manifold and plate positions

labware.description: Labware items are placed on carriers. Defined by container geometry (wells, tubes) and rack layout (rows, columns, spacing).

micro_plate.description: Standard micro plate in ANSI/SLAS format

tip_rack.description: Disposable tip rack in ANSI/SLAS format

nested_tip_rack.requires_transport: iSWAP or CO-RE Gripper to remove empty frames

height_parameters.z_top: Top of container above deck (used for approach)
height_parameters.z_travel: Safe height for lateral movement (145mm traverse)

labware_properties.barcode: Optional barcode on labware for identification

sequences.description: Sequences define the order in which wells/positions on labware are accessed. They are the bridge between the deck layout and the VENUS method steps.

properties.labware_reference: Which labware on which carrier at which track
properties.current_position: Index into the sequence (advances as channels process)

stamp_tool.description: Tool for creating sequences in the VENUS editor

tip_waste.description: Collection station for used disposable tips
tip_waste.location: Fixed position on deck (typically rightmost tracks)

types.tip_waste_chute: Funnel to external container below instrument

liquid_classes.description: Liquid classes define a complete set of aspiration and dispense parameters optimized for a specific liquid type. They control pipetting speed, air gaps, LLD settings, and monitoring. Managed via the CO-RE Liquid Editor in VENUS.

concept.definition: A liquid class is a named parameter set that tells the firmware HOW to aspirate and dispense a specific liquid type. The same volume command (C0AS with av=1000) produces very different physical behavior depending on the liquid class parameters.

hierarchy.liquid_type: Water, DMSO, Ethanol, Blood, Serum, etc.
hierarchy.tip_type: Parameters vary per tip size (10uL vs 1000uL)
hierarchy.dispense_mode: Jet vs Surface vs Blow-out, each with different params
hierarchy.volume_range: Some params change with volume (low-volume corrections)

concept.venus_integration: In VENUS, the liquid class is selected per step (Aspirate/Dispense). VENUS translates the liquid class + volume + tip type into the correct FW command parameters.

aspiration_speed_01ul_s.effect: Slower = less shear, better for cells; Faster = quicker but may cause bubbles

transport_air_01ul.description: Air gap above liquid column to prevent dripping during transport

blow_out_air_01ul.description: Air pushed through to expel remaining liquid during blow-out

pre_wetting_volume_01ul.description: Volume aspirated and dispensed back before actual aspiration (coats tip interior)

settling_time_01s.description: Wait time after positioning before aspirating

immersion_depth_01mm.description: How far below detected liquid surface to position tip

surface_following_01mm.description: Tip follows falling liquid surface during aspiration

values.0: Partial jet (dispense portion, no blow-out, no tip touch)
values.1: Blow-out jet (full dispense with blow-out, no touch)
values.2: Partial surface (dispense portion, touch liquid surface)
values.3: Blow-out surface (full dispense, blow-out, touch surface)

cutoff_speed_01ul_s.description: Plunger speed at the end of dispense (for precise cutoff)

stop_back_volume_01ul.description: Small reverse plunger movement after dispense to prevent dripping

side_touch_off.description: Touch tip against vessel wall after dispense to break droplet

tadm.description: Total Aspiration and Dispense Monitoring parameters

standard_liquid_classes.description: Hamilton ships standard liquid classes for common liquids. Custom classes can be derived.

Water_HighVolumeJet_Empty.use_case: Standard water transfer with full blow-out

Serum_HighVolumeSurface_Empty.use_case: Serum transfer (tendency to foam, surface dispense)
